title: What to Do When a Broker (Delala) Misrepresents an Apartment's Parking and Moving Truck Access
excerpt: Delala broker misrepresentation: false parking and truck access claims, tenant remedies, move-day surprises, Addis Ababa apartment hunting, protecting yourself.
content:
What to do when a broker (delala) misrepresents an apartment's parking and moving truck access

Delala promises "truck to door" and move day reveals alley, no parking, and security refusing 6-meter vehicle—your lease signed, deposit paid, and mover standby ticking. Misrepresented parking and truck access is common Addis friction; response determines whether you absorb shuttle cost or push back on broker and landlord with evidence.

This guide covers remedies when delala misrepresents apartment parking and moving truck access.

Red flags at viewing—verify yourself

  • Walk truck route from nearest legal park to door—measure distance.
  • Ask compound guard where last move parked—guards know truth.
  • Photograph gate height and turn radius; note stairs versus elevator.
  • Visit same weekday hour as planned move—not Sunday quiet lie.
  • Get parking claim in writing on listing message—not verbal only.

If already leased and misled

Document delala's written claims screenshots. Written complaint to landlord—access materially affects habitability and move cost. Request rent adjustment or shuttle cost contribution—negotiation before litigation.

Move day damage control

Book shuttle-aware mover quote revision early—Active Movers survey photos justify carry surcharge. Traffic officer for temporary staging if police cooperate—never illegal block without plan.

Broker accountability

Demand delala fee refund partial if provably false—social pressure and association complaint sometimes work. Future tenants: warn in neighborhood groups factual not libelous.

Legal angles

Consult lawyer if fraudulent misrepresentation induced lease—remedies vary. Keep all messages; witness statements from guard.

Prevention for next lease

Independent surveyor or mover pre-lease walk cheap versus surprise shuttle. Lease clause requesting landlord confirm truck access representation—rare but powerful.

Insurance note

Owner-packed long carry damage harder to claim—professional pack recommended when access difficult.

Conclusion

When delala misrepresents parking and truck access, document claims, negotiate with landlord, plan shuttle honestly, and pursue broker accountability—never sign next lease without guard-validated truck path.
author: Active Movers Team
